title: Poriyal (Michelin-Style)

ingredients.title: Vegetables
items: ½ cup finely chopped bell peppers (red/yellow for color)
Microgreens or finely chopped coriander for garnish

ingredients.title: Tempering & Flavor Layering
items: 1 tsp freshly ground black pepper (optional, for depth)

instruction.title: 1. Prepare Vegetables with Precision
steps: Blanch beans and carrots separately in salted boiling water for 1–2 minutes until just tender. Shock in ice water to retain bright colors. Drain well.
Lightly sauté bell peppers in a dry pan with a touch of coconut oil for 30 seconds to preserve crunch.

instruction.title: 2. Build the Tempering
steps: Heat coconut oil on medium-low heat.
Add mustard seeds; once they pop, add urad dal and chana dal, frying until golden.
Add red chili, green chili, ginger, curry leaves, and hing. Sauté gently for 30–40 seconds to release aroma without burning.

instruction.title: 3. Introduce Vegetables Gradually
steps: Add blanched vegetables and bell peppers to the pan.
Sprinkle turmeric and salt, tossing gently. Cook for 2–3 minutes, just to marry flavors. Keep texture crisp-tender.

instruction.title: 4. Enhance Aroma and Depth
steps: Add freshly grated coconut and toasted sesame seeds off-heat, folding in gently.
Finish with a squeeze of lime juice and a few twists of black pepper for subtle complexity.

instruction.title: 5. Presentation – Restaurant Style
steps: Plate neatly using a ring mold or by stacking vegetables for height.
Garnish with microgreens or finely chopped coriander, and a tiny pinch of extra coconut for visual contrast.

chefTips: Heat control: Keep the pan medium-low when tempering delicate spices to avoid bitterness.
Color & texture: Blanch vegetables separately to prevent overcooking and maintain vibrant colors.
Flavor layering: Add aromatics like ginger, black pepper, and lime at different stages to create depth.
Plating: Height and contrast elevate the dish visually—think vertical stack, not just a pile.
